title: Cohort Definitions
description: Cohort Definitions allow researchers to specify inclusion and exclusion criteria using clinical concepts, temporal logic, and demographic filters to identify patient populations of interest. Cohorts form the foundation for all downstream analyses, including incidence rates, characterisation, and predictive modelling.
tips: Use concept sets to group clinically related terms and apply them consistently across multiple cohort definitions.
Apply index event criteria carefully to ensure the cohort entry date accurately reflects the clinical event of interest.
Review cohort attrition reports to understand how each inclusion criterion reduces the eligible population.
Version your cohort definitions before making changes so that prior analysis results remain reproducible.
Share cohort definitions with collaborators via the Phenotype Library to promote reuse of validated phenotypes.
